commit.message:
Update Code Security SCA Page (#35113)

* Update Code Security SCA Page

This PR updates the Software Composition Analysis (SCA) documentation to make the product model and UI views easier to understand.

Changes:
1. Adds a How it works section that explains Static SCA (repo scans) vs Runtime SCA (APM) and how advisory ingestion impacts results.
2. Reorganizes content into Key capabilities with clearer user workflows (prioritize vulnerabilities, view by repository, PR Gates, library inventory, APM context).
3. Adds Understanding SCA views to explain the difference between Repositories Explorer (commit-scoped snapshot) and Vulnerabilities Explorer (continuously matched), including an example.
4. Documents retroactive advisory matching so customers understand why new CVEs can appear without rerunning scans.
5. Keeps existing setup and language support references and updates “Next steps” accordingly.

* Update content/en/security/code_security/software_composition_analysis/_index.md

commit.message:
refactor: consolidate MCP tools and shorten parameter descriptions

Reduce tool schema footprint by ~1,000 tokens per session:

Phase 1 — Parameter description deduplication:
- Standardize jobId, buildId, pattern, workItem descriptions across all tools
- Fix wording drift between similar parameters

Phase 2 — Tool consolidation (24 → 21 tools):
- helix_download + helix_download_url → helix_download (optional url param)
- helix_search_log + helix_search_file → helix_search_log (optional fileName param)
- azdo_search_log + azdo_search_log_across_steps → azdo_search_log (optional logId)

Unified return types use single-item lists for the 1-vs-many case
rather than discriminated unions. CLI commands consolidated to match.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add direction badges to distinguish request/response/client messages

Each message card now shows its communication direction:
- 📤 REQUEST — system, user, tool results (sent to OpenAI)
- 📥 RESPONSE — assistant, tool_calls, structured (received from OpenAI)
- 🖥️ CLIENT — handoff context, tool execution (runs locally)
- 🖥️ → 📤 — tool results (executed locally, then sent to API)

Payload toggles are color-coded per direction (blue/green/orange)
with matching border colors on the expanded payload blocks.
Dark mode (slate theme) adjustments included.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add interactive message flow visualizations to all exercises

Adds 13 interactive step-through widgets across 9 exercise doc files:
- 00_setup: 1 widget (isolated, 3 steps)
- 01_llm_basics: 3 widgets (growing, independent, structured)
- 02_tool_use: 2 widgets (single function call, multi-iteration loop)
- 03_single_agent: 1 widget (growing, 9-step multi-turn)
- 04_sequential: 1 widget (structured 3-stage pipeline)
- 05_concurrent: 1 widget (isolated fan-out + aggregation)
- 06_group_chat: 2 widgets (brainstorm + maker-checker)
- 07_handoff: 1 widget (structured triage + specialist)
- 08_magentic: 1 widget (task-ledger manager-worker, 7 steps)

Widget content informed by actual exercise runs against Azure OpenAI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: bump version to 0.3.3

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: handle corrupt cache entries gracefully with TryDeserialize

Replace all bare JsonSerializer.Deserialize<T> calls on cache reads with
a TryDeserialize<T> helper that catches JsonException and returns default.
Corrupt entries are treated as cache misses — the next successful API fetch
overwrites them via INSERT OR REPLACE. Entries expire naturally via TTL
(max 4h) if the API also returns null.

Root cause: legacy cache entries from before the \0raw\n format prefix
could crash DeserializeLogContent with JsonException.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Sync eng/common directory with azure-sdk-tools for PR 14461 (#48383)

* Add DirectoryFilterPattern parameter to regeneration pipeline

Add DirectoryFilterPattern parameter to archetype-typespec-emitter.yml
and New-RegenerateMatrix.ps1 to allow filtering the regeneration matrix
by package directory name pattern.

This enables azure-sdk-for-net's http-client-csharp-mgmt CI to only
regenerate Azure.ResourceManager* SDKs instead of all TypeSpec SDKs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Handle empty filter results with array wrapping and early error

Address review feedback: wrap filtered results in @() to ensure
reliable .Count, and fail fast with a clear error when no directories
match the filter pattern.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Support comma-separated filter patterns

Update DirectoryFilterPattern to split on commas and match any pattern,
so callers can filter to multiple SDK prefixes (e.g.,
'Azure.ResourceManager*,Azure.Provisioning*').

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Wei Hu <live1206@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Movement checks (#170)

* Replace wall collision penalty with proactive movement masking

- Add Room.can_link_move_from() for pixel-level movement checks using
  NES ObjGridOffset (tile checks only fire at grid boundaries)
- Add ZeldaGame.can_link_move() with locked-door-with-key override
- Add link_grid_offset to data.json (NES RAM 94)
- Remove WALL_COLLISION_PENALTY and _did_link_run_into_wall from critics
- Replace reactive _handle_wall_bump/move_mask in action_space with
  proactive per-direction can_link_move() calls in the mask builder
- Remove prevent_wall_bumping parameter from ZeldaActionSpace
- Replace stuck_count>=8 heuristic in frame_skip_wrapper with
  deterministic can_link_move_from() check at movement start
- Update tests: wall collision test → movement masking test,
  adjust stalfos injury timing for fewer wasted frames

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix movement masking: always check tiles regardless of grid_offset

ObjGridOffset is axis-agnostic — a nonzero offset from vertical movement
was incorrectly allowing horizontal moves into walls. The tile check is
conservative and never produces false positives, so always apply it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Always build Room from current RAM tiles to fix stale door corridors

ZeldaGame.room now always calls Room.create() with current_tiles instead
of preferring the cache via Room.get(). Room.create() short-circuits with
torch.equal() when tiles haven't changed, so the common case is cheap.

This fixes stale corridor tiles when doors open (key used or enemies
cleared) — the cached Room previously retained the locked-door tile
layout, causing movement masking to block valid corridor movement.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Allow movement into cave entry tiles for proactive masking

Cave top tile (0xF3) is above OW_FIRST_UNWALKABLE but the NES triggers
a cave transition via CheckPassiveTileObjects (Z_07.asm:2957-2962) when
Link walks into it.  Treat it as walkable for movement masking so the
agent can enter caves.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restrict locked-door-with-key override to doorway corridor

The NES CheckDoorway (Z_05.asm:3755) only fires when Link's
perpendicular coordinate matches the doorway: X=$78 for N/S doors,
Y=$8D for E/W doors.  Without this position check, any room with a
locked door would allow movement toward the door from anywhere in the
room, even when blocked by walls/blocks (e.g. debug_1_45.state).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix empty wavefront when exit seed is blocked by obstacles

The wavefront used a single center exit tile offset off-screen as its
BFS seed.  If obstacles blocked expansion from that one point, the
wavefront never reached the rest of the room (e.g. debug_0_68.state).

Two fixes:
- Use ALL exit tiles as off-screen seeds so the BFS can expand from
  every exit point, not just the center.
- Skip can_move checks when expanding from off-screen seeds since they
  are virtual positions with no real NES tiles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update scenarios

* Fix pixel-alignment bug in can_link_move_from using exact NES hotspots

can_link_move_from was delegating to can_move which used approximate tile-
coordinate offsets (tr+1 for feet). The NES computes hotspot pixel positions
directly: base_Y = ObjY + $0B, then subtracts $40 for the status bar before
dividing by 8 to get the tile row.

The old formula (py - 56) // 8 + 1 disagreed with the NES formula
(py - 53) // 8 for 62% of Y positions (when (py-64) % 8 < 5), checking
one row too low. This caused false positives — allowing movement the NES
would block.

Rewrote can_link_move_from to compute exact NES hotspot positions:
- Left:  col=(px-8)//8,   row=(py-53)//8
- Right: col=(px+16)//8,  row=(py-53)//8
- Up:    col=px//8,        row=(py-61)//8,  checks col and col+1
- Down:  col=px//8,        row=(py-45)//8,  checks col and col+1

can_move (tile-space) left unchanged for wavefront BFS which is internally
consistent with its approximate offsets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Category and Status management pages (#29)

Implements admin-only Category and Status management pages for Issue #23.

## Backend (Domain layer)
- MediatR Commands for Categories: CreateCategoryCommand, UpdateCategoryCommand, ArchiveCategoryCommand
- MediatR Queries for Categories: GetCategoriesQuery, GetCategoryByIdQuery
- MediatR Commands for Statuses: CreateStatusCommand, UpdateStatusCommand, ArchiveStatusCommand
- MediatR Queries for Statuses: GetStatusesQuery, GetStatusByIdQuery
- FluentValidation validators for create/update commands

## Services (Web layer)
- CategoryService: Facade for category operations via MediatR
- StatusService: Facade for status operations via MediatR

## Admin Pages (Blazor)
- /admin - Updated dashboard with navigation cards
- /admin/categories - Full CRUD for categories with archive/restore
- /admin/statuses - Full CRUD for statuses with archive/restore
- AdminPageLayout.razor - Shared admin navigation component

## Features
- Create, edit, archive/restore categories and statuses
- Show archived toggle filter
- Success/error message feedback
- Modal forms with validation
- Dark mode support
- Tailwind CSS styling consistent with existing pages
- Admin-only authorization via AdminPolicy

Closes #23

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix security vulnerabilities in auth endpoints (closes #13) (#15)

Security Fixes:
- Add open redirect prevention: Validate returnUrl is local path before redirecting
- Change logout from GET to POST: Prevents CSRF logout attacks
- Update LoginDisplay.razor: Use form with POST method and AntiforgeryToken

Implementation Details:
- Added IsLocalUrl() helper to validate URLs start with / but not //
- Rejects http://, https://, and protocol-relative URLs
- Logout endpoint now requires POST method
- Antiforgery validation via UseAntiforgery() middleware

Testing:
- Added Web.Tests project with integration tests
- Tests verify URL validation logic for open redirect protection
- Tests verify GET logout returns 404 (endpoint removed)
- Tests verify POST logout requires authentication
- All 12 tests passing

References:
- OWASP Open Redirect: https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Unvalidated_Redirects_and_Forwards_Cheat_Sheet.html

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- DTOs: IssueDto, CategoryDto, StatusDto, CommentDto, UserDto, PaginatedResponse

- Repository updated to use new Result pattern
- DbContext configured (no Users DbSet - Auth0 sourced)

Closes #6

feat: Implement Auth0 authentication (#12)

Adds Auth0 authentication with role-based authorization.

- Added Auth0Options configuration class
- Implemented Admin and User authorization policies
- Created AuthorizationRoles constants
- Added LoginDisplay component for authentication UI
- Added CascadingAuthenticationState to App.razor
- Created example protected pages (Admin.razor, Issues.razor)
- Configured HTTPS and antiforgery tokens
- Added comprehensive Auth/README.md documentation
- Auth0 config uses placeholders (secrets via user-secrets/Key Vault)

Closes #5
